title: AI-Assisted Development Practical
description: Hands-on exercises for AI-assisted full-stack development using Claude Code with a React + Express + Prisma monorepo

setup.install: npm install && npm run db:migrate:dev -w backend && npm run db:seed -w backend

env.FRONTEND_URL: Backend CORS origin for frontend (default: http://localhost:5173)

exercises.title: Task List CRUD
exercises.description: Complete a task management interface by wiring up create, update, and delete operations using AI assistance. The read functionality and backend API are already built.

steps.title: Explore the codebase
steps.instruction: Ask Claude: 'Explain how the task list exercise works — walk me through the frontend component, the API routes, and the database model'

steps.title: Connect the New Task button
steps.instruction: Ask Claude to help you connect the 'New Task' button in the ExerciseTaskList component to the existing POST /api/exercises/tasks endpoint, including a form for task name, description, and priority

steps.title: Implement task status updates
steps.instruction: Ask Claude to add the ability to toggle task status (e.g., mark as complete) by calling the PUT /api/exercises/tasks/:id endpoint

steps.title: Implement task deletion
steps.instruction: Ask Claude to add a delete button to each task row that calls the DELETE /api/exercises/tasks/:id endpoint with a confirmation dialog

steps.title: Add task editing
steps.instruction: Ask Claude to replace the 'View' button with an 'Edit' button that opens an inline form or modal for editing task details via the PUT endpoint

exercises.title: Analytics Chart
exercises.description: Fetch monthly analytics data from the backend API and visualize it using a charting library. You'll need to discover the API endpoint and choose how to display the data — all with AI assistance.

steps.title: Discover the API
steps.instruction: Ask Claude: 'I need to display analytics data in the AnalyticsChart exercise. Can you find the backend API endpoint that serves monthly analytics data and explain what data it returns?'

steps.title: Fetch the data
steps.instruction: Ask Claude to add a useEffect hook to the AnalyticsChartExercise component that fetches the analytics data from the API endpoint and stores it in state

steps.title: Render the chart
steps.instruction: Ask Claude: 'Look at the example charts in the Example Layouts section of this app and help me implement a similar chart in the AnalyticsChartExercise component to display page views and total visits over time'

steps.title: Enhance the visualisation
steps.instruction: Ask Claude to improve the chart with additional metrics, better formatting, or interactive features like tooltips and legends

exercises.title: LLM Integration
exercises.description: Build a full-stack LLM query interface — create a backend API endpoint that calls an LLM provider, wire it to the frontend form, and add model selection. This exercise covers API design, SDK integration, and UI development.

steps.title: Plan the implementation
steps.instruction: Ask Claude: 'I need to implement the LLM Query exercise. Look at the existing LlmQuery component and help me create a plan for building the backend endpoint and connecting it to the frontend'

steps.title: Create the backend endpoint
steps.instruction: Ask Claude to create a new API endpoint at POST /api/llm/query that accepts a user query, calls an LLM provider (e.g., Anthropic Claude API), and returns the response. Follow the existing backend patterns for routes, controllers, and services

steps.title: Connect the frontend
steps.instruction: Ask Claude to update the handleSubmit function in the LlmQuery component to call your new backend endpoint and display the LLM's response below the form, including loading and error states

steps.title: Add model selection
steps.instruction: Ask Claude to add a dropdown to the LLM Query UI that lets the user select which model to use, and pass that selection to the backend endpoint
